title: D2 JWT claim names spec
description: Single source of truth for every JWT claim name D2 reads or writes across the runtime. Includes standard OAuth/OIDC claims (sub, aud, iat, ...) AND D2-specific claims (d2_session_id, d2_org_id, ...) AND inside-act claims (d2_kind nested under the act object). Codegen emits matching JwtClaimTypes catalogs on .NET (DcsvIo.D2.Auth.Abstractions.JwtClaimTypes) and TS (@dcsv-io/d2-auth-abstractions JwtClaimTypes). A separate parity test asserts every IAuthContext claim: annotation references an entry here.

constName.description: Public C# / TS constant identifier — UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.

value.description: Wire-format claim name (the on-the-token string). For inside-act claims this is the nested key under act, e.g. d2_kind.

kind.description: Closed enum classifying the claim. standard = OAuth/OIDC vocabulary; d2-custom = D2-specific top-level claim (d2_ prefix); inside-act = nested key inside the act object per RFC 8693 §2.1.

description.description: Human-readable description of the claim. Emitted as xmldoc / JSDoc on every catalog the claim appears in.

deprecated.description: When true, the entry is deprecated: the generated C# constant + factory carry [Obsolete] and the TS constant + factory carry @deprecated. Additive — the entry is NEVER deleted. Deleting a deprecated (or non-deprecated) entry is a breaking change the JSON-diff gate fails.

deprecatedReason.description: Plain dev-facing English explaining why the code is deprecated; rendered into the [Obsolete("...")] message, the @deprecated JSDoc, and the changelog entry. NOT a TK key (developer-facing, not user-facing).

replacedBy.description: Wire code of the successor entry (e.g. RESOURCE_NOT_FOUND). Must reference a real, non-deprecated entry in the merged registry. Omit for pure retirement with no replacement.

sunset.description: ISO-8601 date for the future RFC 8594 Sunset response header. Edge-runtime-deferred: carried on the contract now, consumed by the Edge response middleware when it exists. Inert until then.
